hero.title: Christian Festa
hero.subTitle: Technology executive with {YEARS} years across the full software lifecycle, from hands-on development and system integration to directing multi-million-dollar design, development, and integration (DDI) programs. I lead engineering and design teams of 30+ and build secure generative AI on AWS, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems that let teams query proprietary data in plain language without ever exposing it. I came up as a developer and a UX designer, and I still think like both, which keeps technical decisions grounded, user-focused, and tied to what the business actually needs.

roles.title: AI & Cloud Innovation Leader
roles.subTitle: I direct generative AI on AWS, building secure Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems that let users query proprietary data in plain language without exposing PII or IP. They run on Amazon Bedrock with isolated foundation models and guardrails that hold up even against edge-case prompts.

roles.title: Director of Software Engineering
roles.subTitle: I own all design, development, and integration (DDI) across the full lifecycle, from architecture through deployment. I scale cross-functional teams, accelerate delivery with modern tooling and disciplined Agile, and keep technical strategy tied to business outcomes.

roles.title: Principal UX Designer
roles.subTitle: I build design systems and UX strategy for complex, accessible, enterprise-scale interfaces. My Figma-led initiatives have doubled design-team output, enforced WCAG compliance, and produced interfaces that hold up across every device and handoff.

experience.title: Director of Software Engineering
experience.description: As Director of Software Engineering, Technical Delivery Manager, and Principal Design Manager, I own all design, development, and integration (DDI) for a $40M AWS-hosted public and administrative portal, while building the organization's secure generative AI capability. I direct cross-functional teams of 30+ designers, developers, and engineers across the full lifecycle.
bullets: Leading the build of a multi-tenant platform engineered to support a portfolio of products on shared infrastructure, currently moving toward market launch.

Led strategy and delivery of a secure Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application on Amazon Bedrock, letting users query proprietary company data through natural-language chat with zero exposure of PII or IP, even under edge-case prompts, using isolated foundation models and layered guardrails.
Built the governance framework for responsible AI adoption, balancing fast iteration against enterprise compliance and giving the organization a repeatable model for future AI initiatives.

Stood up a Figma-based design system that standardized components, enforced WCAG compliance, and guaranteed responsive behavior across devices, streamlining design-to-development handoff and code review.
Directed onshore and offshore teams through the full project lifecycle, instituting testing workflows that took the first phase live in just three months.
Mentored designers at every level and managed stakeholder relationships through weekly reviews and progress reporting, keeping creative vision aligned with business goals.
Drove post-launch optimization from user feedback and performance metrics, sharpening operational efficiency and making the platform a cornerstone of the organization's digital strategy.

experience.title: Technical Program and Design Manager
experience.description: As Technical Program Manager and Design Manager, I directed multiple workstreams across three of four solutions in a $100M Google Cloud Platform-hosted DDI program, leading 30+ designers, developers, business analysts, and testers across onshore and offshore teams.
bullets: Drove early Figma adoption and built a comprehensive design system that more than doubled design-team output and accelerated the design process 2x.
Set UI/UX standards for consistency, WCAG compliance, and cross-device responsiveness, embedding them across the lifecycle from requirements gathering to deployment.
Ran UX design processes, stakeholder workshops, and team coordination to ship a scalable, user-friendly portal that expanded customer-service capabilities.
Aligned deliverables to organizational goals, keeping execution efficient and built for long-term scale.

experience.title: Technical Consultant
experience.organization: County Welfare Directors Association of California
experience.description: As a Technical Consultant on the Independent Verification & Validation (IV&V) team, I validated a complex multi-system integration for a high-profile public-sector initiative as the DDI program moved into long-term maintenance and operations (M&O).
bullets: Participated in system design and analyzed technical documentation to surface integration risks and bottlenecks before they could threaten timelines or performance.
Reviewed and signed off on technical deliverables, recommending process improvements that resolved issues early rather than after escalation.
Presented findings in biweekly reviews with developers and stakeholders, securing buy-in and bridging technical and non-technical teams.
Helped deliver a reliable, fully integrated system that met every contractual obligation and became a model for later initiatives.

experience.title: Technical Consultant
experience.description: I joined as a college new hire and grew into a management role, working across criminal-justice, welfare, and grant-management systems and wearing every hat from UX design through development, integration, and maintenance. This is where my UX career began, designing the wireframes that developers built from.
bullets: Launched my UX career here, designing the wireframes and interaction flows developers built from and translating complex public-sector requirements into clear, usable interfaces.
Set standards and protocols for multi-system case exchange, enabling seamless data sharing, and led implementation builds through to deployment.
Designed and built in-house tools that streamlined workflows, cut errors, and reduced task completion time from hours to minutes.
Built and maintained regression suites, running rigorous testing to keep systems reliable under load.
Co-authored winning business-development proposals, helping secure $100M+ in contracts.
Managed teams of up to 25 through full system builds, configurations, third-party integrations, and user-acceptance testing.

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Information Sciences and Technology
education.description: Built the foundation for my career in IT, systems integration, software development, and human-computer interaction. Graduated with honors.

education.title: Project Management Professional (PMP) Certification
education.description: Formal grounding in Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, risk management, and stakeholder engagement, giving me the toolkit to deliver multi-million-dollar projects on time, on budget, and beyond expectations.

education.title: Google User Experience (UX) Design Certification
education.description: Hands-on training in user research, wireframing, prototyping, and usability testing that formalized the design practice I had been building on the job since 2009.

projects.name: Walkout Intros
projects.description: Pro-quality walkout intros for sports teams. Build a roster, pair each player with an AI-announcer intro and a trimmed music cue, then trigger their entrance with a tap. Works with or without Apple Music.
